data.title: SEC Finally Specifies Proof-of-Work Mining Does Not Involve Securities Offering
data.description:
The statement brings legal certainty against the murky argument that crypto mining satisfied the Howey Test.

data.title: Eric Trump Joins Metaplanet Advisory Board as Trump Family Deepens Crypto Ties
data.description:
President Trump's family continues to deepen its direct stake in the crypto industry as he continues to advocate for it politically.

data.title: Ethereum Fees Drop by 50% Amid Decline in On-Chain Activity
data.description: According to BlockBeats, IntoTheBlock reported a significant decrease in Ethereum fees this week, with a 50% drop attributed to a continued slowdown in on-chain activity. This decline reflects a broader trend of reduced transactions and interactions on the Ethereum network, impacting the overall fee structure.

data.title: Kaito founder Yu Hu has used his own funds to buy and pledge 1 million KAITO
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Kaito founder Yu Hu said on the social platform that in the past few days he has used his own funds TWAP to buy 1 million KAITO (about 1.4 million US dollars) and has pledged all of them, making him the second largest KAITO pledger on the chain.
He also set up a self-locking mechanism based on Kaito protocol revenue: 50% will be unlocked when the protocol revenue reaches 50 million US dollars, and all will be unlocked when it reaches 100 million US dollars. Yu Hu will elaborate on his operating motivation and his views on the long-term development of Kaito tomorrow.

data.title: Chinese couple linked to a crypto scam, kidnapping arrested in Thailand
data.description:
The Thai police have arrested a Chinese woman in connection with a crypto investment scam. Surprisingly, they have found out that her boyfriend, who was located at the same residence, is a fugitive sought in China for alleged kidnapping. According to reports,  Wu Di, a 27-year-old Chinese woman, was arrested on Wednesday by the Technology […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/chinese-couple-linked-crypto-scam-kidnapping/

data.title: PAC Fairshake makes emergency funding injection to Florida Republican candidates
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Fairshake, a super political action committee (PAC) representing the crypto industry, recently invested more than $1.5 million in advertising support funds for Republican candidates in two special elections for the U.S. House of Representatives in Florida. These two seats are key to the Republican Party maintaining a slim majority in the House of Representatives.
Among them, Fairshake invested about $1.2 million in state senator Randy Fine, who ran for the vacant seat of former National Security Advisor Michael Waltz, and about $345,000 in state treasurer Jimmy Patronis, who ran for the vacant seat of Matt Gaetz. Both are considered candidates who support crypto assets.
The by-election will be held on April 1, and early voting will begin this weekend. The Democratic Party is also actively investing, but the relevant constituencies have mostly been Republican-dominated areas in past elections. (CoinDesk)

data.title: TON Foundation clarifies: It did not "raise" $400 million, some venture capital institutions hold Toncoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The TON Foundation issued a statement today to clarify some media reports that "TON Foundation raised $400 million". The foundation stated that it did not conduct relevant financing activities, but disclosed that more than $400 million worth of Toncoin is currently held by several leading US venture capital institutions.

data.title: FTX related addresses transferred out $3.3 million worth of SOL
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Arkham monitoring, an FTX-related address made an on-chain transfer today, transferring out SOL worth about $3.3 million. This address had previously frequently transferred assets, and the community is closely watching its subsequent movements.

data.title: Tornado Cash related addresses have been removed from the blacklist
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Tornado Cash related addresses have been removed from the blacklist.
